text.title: Structured Logging Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying logging-patterns. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: log.info('user ' + email + ' logged in') in a code path; aggregator can't query by user.
antipattern.fix: Replace with log.info('user_login', user_id=..., email=mask(email)). Add lint to flag string concatenation in log calls.

antipattern.symptom: Multi-service trace impossible because logs lack correlation_id; root-cause analysis takes hours.
antipattern.root-cause: Middleware not wired; child contexts didn't inherit.
antipattern.fix: Add request-id middleware that generates / propagates correlation_id. Bind to logger context via contextvars / async-local-storage.

antipattern.symptom: Error log captures the full request body including credit-card number; aggregator stores it indefinitely.
antipattern.fix: Make redaction recursive over the payload. Add a unit test that asserts each PII field is masked in nested contexts.

antipattern.symptom: `print('here')` left in a hot path; logs are noisy + un-redacted.
antipattern.root-cause: Author used print for local debug; never removed.
antipattern.fix: Add ruff T20 / no-console / forbidigo rule. Run pre-commit. Sweep existing prints.
